title: Dynamic Alerting Platform — RBAC Schema (_rbac.yaml, ADR-027 / LD-6)
description: JSON Schema (draft-07) for validating _rbac.yaml — the tenant-api authorization boundary. Mirrors the STRICT (yaml KnownFields) parser in components/tenant-api/internal/rbac/rbac.go (RBACConfig / GroupRule / MatchBlock): an unknown key (e.g. `permissons:` typo) that the runtime rejects as a load error is caught here at author / CI time instead. The `permissions` enum is a deliberately STRICTER authoring guard than the parser (Permission is an unconstrained string and validateConfig has no enum check, so `[readonly]` would parse fine and silently grant nothing) — it can only reject typos, never a legitimate permission. The guiding rule is: no LOOSER than the parser — the schema never accepts a config the parser load-REJECTS (so present-but-null values, which yaml decodes to nil slices without error, are accepted; and match.claims value lists carry the same non-empty/non-blank constraints validateConfig enforces). It may be deliberately STRICTER only as an authoring hygiene guard that can reject a typo but never a legitimate value: the permission enum, and non-blank list items (a blank org-scope / tenant / claim value is inert in Go but almost always a mistake). SoT = the Go structs; keep this schema in sync when a GroupRule / MatchBlock field is added. Cross-field fail-closed rules (an empty `match: {}` is a load error; an org-scope / match.claims key must be declared in --identity-claim-headers) are NOT expressible in JSON Schema and stay enforced by the parser — this schema guards shape / key-typo / enum only.

groups.title: RBAC Group Rules
groups.description: List of group rules. An absent / null / empty list parses to zero groups (MED-8 fail-closed: all access denied unless --rbac-empty-open).

groupRule.title: Group Rule
groupRule.description: One authorization rule. Without `match` the `name` is the IdP-group matcher (legacy shape, byte-identical to pre-P3); with `match` the `name` becomes a pure label / audit identifier.

org-scope.title: Org-scope claim key
org-scope.description: Opt-in org-scope axis (ADR-027 / LD-6 P4): the claim KEY whose caller value must be one of the target tenant's orgs (from _tenant_orgs.yaml). Must be declared in --identity-claim-headers (parser-enforced). Null / absent = the rule places no org restriction; an empty string is rejected as a meaningless value.

matchBlock.title: Claims-aware matcher (LD-6 P3)
matchBlock.description: AND across condition kinds, OR within a list. A present-but-null `match:` IS a load error (detectNullMatchBlocks) so null is not accepted here; an empty match block is likewise a load error — a cross-field rule this schema cannot express, enforced by the parser.

claims.title: Claim key → allowed values
claims.description: Each claim key maps to a NON-EMPTY list of NON-BLANK allowed values (exact string match, OR-within); keys AND-across. validateConfig load-rejects an empty value list and a blank value (rbac.go), so the schema matches — mirroring the match.groups constraints.
